items.name: Acetylsalicylic Acid (Aspirin)
items.indication: Suspected myocardial ischaemia
items.contraindications: - Known or suspected allergy to salicylates; - Known or suspected active bleeding; - Known bleeding tendency; - Current psychostimulant overdose (due to an increased risk of cerebral haemorrhage)
items.adverse_effects: - Allergic reactions; - Aggravation of bleeding tendencies; - Gastric irritation (unlikely after single dose)
items.dose:
##### Adult
- 1 tablet (300mg)
- Chewed and swallowed, or dissolved in a small amount of water.
- \xbd tablet (150mg) if on warfarin.

items.name: Adenosine (Adenocor)
items.indication: - Treatment of supraventricular tachycardia.; - Not for the treatment of atrial flutter or fibrillation; however, if mistakenly administered to patients in these arrhythmias, the decrease in AV conduction may unmask atrial activity.
items.adverse_effects: Common, although transient and generally minor:; - Arrhythmias at the time of conversion are common (up to 55% patients) – including PVCs, PACs, sinus bradycardia, AV blocks; - Transient flushing of the skin; - Mild dyspnoea; - Chest tightness; - Nausea; - Headache; - Feelings of apprehension and fear
items.precautions: - Asthma – may exacerbate bronchospasm; - Antagonised by: theophylline (Nuelin); - Potentiated by:; - dipyridamole (Persantin, Asasantin); - carbamazepine (Tegretol, Teril)
items.dose:
##### Adult (symptomatic adults only)
- **ICP: **6mg IV - rapid bolus (over 1 - 2 seconds)
- Give into a fast flowing pump set.
- **ICP: **If first is unsuccessful:
- Give second dose (2 minutes between doses)
- 12mg IV - rapid bolus (over 1 - 2 seconds).##### Paediatric
- **ICP:** First dose: 0.05mg/kg – given as above
- **ICP:** Second dose: 0.1mg/kg

items.name: Adrenaline
items.indication: - Cardiac arrest; - Anaphylaxis; - Severe life-threatening asthma; - Bradyarrhythmias resistant to atropine **- ICP only**; - Severe upper airway swelling; - Shock unresponsive to fluid boluses **- ICP only**
items.contraindications: - Hypovolaemic shock without adequate fluid replacement
items.adverse_effects: - Tachycardia; - Tachyarrhythmias; - Hypertension
items.precautions: These apply to patients with cardiac output only:; - Care with patients with history of hypertension; - Care with patients with history of ischaemic heart disease; - Give extremely slowly to patients on MAO Inhibitor antidepressants (e.g. Nardil, Parnate) as adrenaline may provoke a greatly exaggerated response. Generally, patients on MAOIs with cardiac output should receive no more than \xbc of the normal dose of adrenaline, titrated to response; - Hypothermia
items.dose:
#### Bradyarrhythmias resistant to atropine##### Adult and Paediatric
- **ICP:** IV/IO adrenaline by infusion – titrated to response
#### Cardiac arrest##### Adult
- 1mg IV or IO – fast push
- No limit on number of doses in cardiac arrest.
##### Paediatric
- IV: 0.01mg/kg – fast push
- No limit on number of doses in cardiac arrest.
- **ICP:** IO: 0.01mg/kg – fast push
- No limit on number of doses in cardiac arrest.
- **ICP:** ETT: NEWBORN ONLY
- If no IV or IO access – 0.02mg/kg.
#### Anaphylaxis / asthma (severe life threatening)##### Adult
- IM: 0.5mg
- Repeat 5 minutely (max three doses).
- **ICP:** If required – **IV/IO** adrenaline by infusion
- Titrated to response.
##### Paediatric
- IM: 0.01mg/kg (up to 50kg)
- Repeat 5 minutely (max three doses).
- **ICP:** If required – **IV/IO** adrenaline by infusion
- Titrated to response.
#### Severe upper airway swelling##### Adult
- **IM:** 0.5mg
- Repeat 5 minutely (max three doses).
##### Paediatric
- Weight >10kg: nebulise 5ml adrenaline 1:1,000
- Weight

items.name: Amiodarone (Cordarone)
items.indication: Effective for both supraventricular and ventricular arrhythmias. ACTAS uses:; - To slow the ventricular rate in atrial fibrillation and flutter; - To treat VF and VT
items.contraindications: - Known hypersensitivity; - Cross sensitivity to iodine
items.adverse_effects: When given IV, can cause vasodilation, negative inotropic effects and hypotension (dose and rate dependent).; Occasionally may cause:; - Bradycardia (especially in older patients); - Phlebitis; - Hot flushes / sweating
items.dose:
#### VF/VT cardiac arrest##### Adult
- **ICP:** 300mg IV/IO
- Over 30-60 seconds.
##### Paediatric
- **ICP:** 5mg/kg IV/IO (to maximum 300mg)
- Over 30-60 seconds.
#### VT, AF and A.flutter - with cardiac output##### Adult
- **ICP:** 150mg IV via Springfusor
- Made up to 7ml total volume with normal saline (7ml will run over 10 minutes)
- VT – if extremely compromised, may be given over 5 minutes by slow IV injection
- No repeat dose for either treatment regime.
##### Paediatric
- **ICP:** 5mg/kg IV (to maximum 150mg) via Springfusor
- Made up to 7ml total volume with normal saline (7ml will run over 10 minutes)
- VT – if extremely compromised, may be given over 5 minutes by slow IV injection
- No repeat dose for either treatment regime.

items.name: Atropine Sulphate
items.indication: - Bradyarrhythmias with poor perfusion **- ICP only**; - Organophosphate/ nerve agent poisoning; - Big black spider bite with cholinergic symptoms
items.adverse_effects: - Tachycardia; - Palpitations; - Blurred vision; - Dry mouth; - Confusion; - Urinary retention; - Increased body temperature (by decreasing perspiration)
items.precautions: - Care needed in patients with glaucoma; - Aim not to increase heart rate above 100bpm
items.dose:
#### Bradyarrhythmias##### Adult
- **ICP:** IV / IO: 600mcg – fast push
- Repeat once after 3-5 minutes, if required.
##### Paediatric
Not used
#### Organophosphate / Nerve Agent Poisoning##### Adult
- IV / IO / IM: initial dose 2400mcg – fast push
- **AP: ** IM/IV: initial dose 2400mcg – fast push
- Repeat every 3 – 5 minutes.
- Double each repeat dose as required (e.g. 2.4mg → 4.8mg → 9.6mg etc.). - No upper limit on doses until all management end goals achieved.
- **Note:** Give IM while IV/IO access is being obtained.
##### Paediatric
- IV / IO / IM: initial dose 50mcg/kg (max. initial dose 2400mcg) – fast push
- **AP: ** IM/IV: initial dose 50mcg/kg (max. initial dose 2400mcg) – fast push - Repeat every 3 – 5 minutes.
- Double each repeat dose as required- No upper limit on doses until all management end goals achieved.
- **Note:** Give IM while IV/IO access is being obtained.
- Split larger doses into multiple injections.
**Note:** In an MCI situation where multiple patients require simultaneous management, a simplified repeat dose regimen, where the initial dose is repeated instead of doubled doses, can be used.
#### Big black spider bite with cholinergic symptoms##### Adult
- IV / IO / IM: 600mcg – fast push
- **AP: ** IM/IV: 600mcg – fast push
- No repeat##### Paediatric
- IV / IO / IM: 20mcg/kg (max. 600mcg) – fast push
- **AP: ** IM/IV: 20mcg/kg (max. 600mcg) - fast- No repeat

items.name: Calcium Chloride
items.indication: - Emergency treatment of hyperkalaemia; - Cardiac arrest secondary to hyperkalaemia (i.e., hyperkalaemia is thought to be the cause of the cardiac arrest); - Prolonged hypotension post-magnesium administration, if unresponsive to fluid
items.adverse_effects: - Tissue necrosis if extravasation; - Precipitates out in contact with bicarbonate; - If fast pushed:; - Bradycardia; - Peripheral vasodilation; - Hypotension
items.dose:
##### Adult and Paediatric
- **ICP:**** With cardiac output:** 10mg/kg (up to 1g) IV/IO
- Over 2 minutes
- Repeat once, if required.
- **ICP:**** No cardiac output:** 10mg/kg (up to 1g) IV/IO
- Over 30 – 60 seconds
- Repeat once, if required.

items.name: Ceftriaxone (Rocephin)
items.indication: Suspected meningococcal disease, in acutely ill patients
items.adverse_effects: - Hypersensitivity reactions (not common); - Diarrhoea; - Skin rash
items.precautions: It is possible that 20% of patients with a penicillin allergy may be sensitive to ceftriaxone
items.dose:
##### Adult and Paediatric
- 50mg/kg, to a total of 2g – IV preferred
- Make up to 10ml with normal saline
- Give slowly over 2 - 4 minutes
- May be administered IM: Reconstitute each 1g vial with 3.5ml lignocaine. Draw up dose into two syringes and administer into two separate injection sites.
- **ICP:** May also be given IO if no IV available.

items.name: Droperidol (Dorm)
items.indication: Pharmacological intervention for combative / agitated patients.
items.contraindications: - Known hypersensitivity; - Parkinson’s disease; - Post ictal
items.adverse_effects: - Arrhythmias, including prolonged QT interval or torsades de pointes (all patients receiving droperidol must have ECG monitoring); - Extrapyramidal effects (rare); - Neuroleptic malignant syndrome (rare); - May lower seizure threshold
items.precautions: Patients in severe pain; analgesia should also be provided
items.dose:
##### Adult (>14 years of age)
- 10mg via IM injection only (no IV/IO use)
- Repeat once, after 10-15 minutes, if required.
##### Frail / elderly (>65 years)
- 5mg via IM injection only (no IV/IO use)
- Repeat once, after 10-15 minutes, if required.
##### Paediatric (

items.name: Fentanyl (Intra-nasal)
items.indication: - Analgesic for moderate to severe pain.; - Safe alternative analgesic in patients with known morphine allergy.
items.contraindications: - Known allergy or hypersensitivity; - Bilateral bleeding or occluded nostrils; - Age Increased risk of adverse effects. Consider reducing dose.; - Frail or elderly; Increased risk of adverse effects. Consider reducing dose.; - Reduced level of consciousness; Potential difficulty correctly administering via IN route.
items.adverse_effects: - Reduced conscious state; - Respiratory depression; - Nausea/vomiting; - Bradycardia (rare); - Rigidity of the diaphragm, intercostals and jaw (rare)
items.dose:
#### Analgesia – Intranasal##### Adult and Paediatric
- 1.5mcg/kg IN, up to 100mcg.
- Repeat @5/60 intervals as required.

items.name: Glucagon
items.indication: Treatment of hypoglycaemia (BGL 20kg: 1 IU – IM; - Single dose only.
items.adverse_effects: See clinical management guideline
items.dose: See CMG for dose details

items.name: Glucose 10%
items.indication: Treat hypoglycaemia (BGL
items.adverse_effects: See clinical management guideline
items.dose: See CMG for dose details

items.name: Oral Glucose Gel
items.indication: Treatment of hypoglycaemia (BGL
items.adverse_effects: See clinical management guideline
items.dose: See CMG for dose details

items.name: Glyceryl Trinitrate (Anginine or Nitrolingual Pumpspray)
items.indication: - Relieve cardiac pain of ischaemic origin; - Relieve acute pulmonary oedema; - Management of autonomic dysreflexia
items.contraindications: - Do not administer if systolic BP is
items.adverse_effects: - Hypotension; - Headache; - Flushing of skin; - Occasionally – bradycardia
items.dose: See CMG for dose details

items.name: Heparin
items.indication: Patients with an acute ST elevation myocardial infarction who meet the requirements for early percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) procedure
items.adverse_effects: - Bleeding; - Thrombocytopenia; - Anaphylactoid reactions (rare)
items.precautions: Use with care in patients with extensive history of allergies
items.dose:
##### Adult
- 5000u IV – over 30-60 seconds
##### Paediatric
Not used.

items.name: Hydrocortisone
items.indication: - Moderate to severe respiratory distress due to asthma or exacerbation of COPD; - Patients with a diagnosed adrenal insufficiency who experience a stress response and exhibit altered conscious state and/or refractory hypotension
items.adverse_effects: Nil significant with single use
items.dose:
#### Asthma and COPD##### Adult
- **ICP:** 100mg IV – slowly over 2 minutes
##### Paediatric
- **ICP:** 4mg/kg IV (to a maximum of 100mg) – slowly over 2 minutes
#### Adrenal crisis##### Adult
- **ICP:** 100mg IV – slowly over 2 minutes
- May be administered IM.
##### Paediatric
- **ICP:** IV (slowly over 2 minutes):
- 12 years = 100mg IV.
- **ICP:** May be administered IM.

items.name: Ibuprofen
items.indication: Analgesia for mild-moderate pain due to inflammation
items.contraindications: - Known hypersensitivity to NSAIDs; - Acute asthma; - Dehydration; - Current peptic ulcers; - Known or suspected active bleeding; - Inability to swallow/impaired gag reflex; - End stage renal or hepatic failure; - Acute heart failure; - Previous ibuprofen administration within 4 hours
items.adverse_effects: - Nausea; - Dyspepsia; - GI ulceration or bleeding; - Diarrhoea; - Headache; - Dizziness
items.precautions: - History of peptic ulcers or GI bleeding; - Hepatic impairment; - Asthma; - Cardiovascular disease
items.dose:
##### Adult
- ≥40kg: 400mg
- ≤40kg: 200mg
##### Paediatric
- Tablets: 20-40kg: 200mg
- PO (liquid): Ibuprofen 100mg/5ml
- 3-6 months (6-8 kg): 3 - 4ml
- 6-12 months (8-10 kg): 4 - 5ml- 1-3 years (10-14 kg): 5 - 7ml- 3-5 years (14-18 kg): 7 - 9ml- 5-7 years (18-22 kg): 9 - 11ml- 7-9 years (22-28 kg): 11 - 14ml- 9-12 years (28-40 kg): 14 - 20ml

items.name: Ipratropium Bromide (Atrovent)
items.indication: - Severe respiratory distress due to asthma or COPD
items.adverse_effects: Rare with single use. Occasionally, urine retention.
items.precautions: Care needed with use in patients with glaucoma
items.dose:
#### Severe to life threatening respiratory distress
**pMDI via spacer**
##### Adult ≥6yrs
- 8 actuations
- Repeat once after 20 minutes
- 1 actuation to 4 breaths.
##### Paediatric

items.name: Ketamine Hydrochloride (Ketalar)
items.indication: - Sedation of combative/agitated patients, who are an immediate danger to themselves or others; - Induction agent for intubation **- ICP only**; - Maintenance of sedation **- ICP only**; - Procedural dissociation to facilitate painful interventions (e.g., straightening long bones, extrication) **- ICP only**; - Analgesia for severe pain in the patient with signs of shock, or is at risk of shock **- ICP only**; - Analgesia adjunct for severe unremitting pain 10 minutes post second opioid dose; - Analgesia for severe pain in patients who are unable to have, or have developed a significant tolerance to opioids
items.contraindications: - Known sensitivity; - Active myocardial ischaemia or LVF or symptomatic hypertension (not applicable when used for RSI or to maintain sedation for ETT); - Children 65 years, or with general debility.; - Repeat 1mg/kg IM injection after 5 minutes (if required).
items.adverse_effects: - Laryngospasm; - Hypersalivation; - Dysphoria/emergence reactions; - Muscle twitching and purposeless movements; - Respiratory depression → apnoea (seen in extreme doses or fast administration); - Hypotension (generally seen in hypovolaemic/cardiac depressed patients)
items.dose: See CMG for dose details

items.name: Levetiracetam
items.indication: Status epilepticus:; - Refractory seizure activity 5/60 after second benzodiazepine dose.; - >20/60 seizure duration.
items.adverse_effects: - Behavioural effects; - Drowsiness; - Weakness; - Dizziness and vertigo; - Headache; - Insomnia; - Amnesia; - Ataxia; - Diplopia; - Anorexia
items.dose:
- **ICP:** 40mg/kg (max. 3000mg) IV/IO, infused over 5/60 via infusion pump, no repeat.
**Note:** If infusion pump unavailable, dose can be administered via slow IV/IO push over 5/60.

items.name: Lignocaine
items.indication: - Local anaesthesia prior to invasive procedures; - VT with cardiac output, in patients who cannot have amiodarone; - Flushing of a painful IO in an aware patient; - Solvent for IM ceftriaxone administration
items.adverse_effects: These effects are extremely unlikely in usual subcutaneous doses, especially if the syringe is continually aspirated. More likely if given IV.; - CNS effects:; stimulation followed by depression, drowsiness, agitation, muscle twitching, seizures, coma; - Cardiac effects:; hypotension, bradycardia, heart block, asystole
items.dose:
#### Local anaesthesia##### Adult and Paediatric
- **ICP:** Up to 5ml subcutaneously
#### VT with cardiac output##### Adult and Paediatric
- **ICP:** 1mg/kg – slow IV / IO over 2 minutes
#### IO in an aware patient##### Adult
- **ICP:** 40mg through primed IO extension tubing, slowly over 2 minutes
- Allow lignocaine to dwell in IO space for 60 seconds, then flush with 5-10ml of normal saline
- Repeat after 5 minutes if needed: 20mg over 60 seconds (max. 2 x repeats)
- Total max. dose 80mg
- Consider systemic pain relief for patients not responding to IO lignocaine##### Paediatric
- **ICP:** 0.5mg/kg (to max. 40mg) through primed IO extension tubing, slowly over 2 minutes
- Allow lignocaine to dwell in IO space for 60 seconds, then flush with 5-10ml of normal saline
- Repeat after 5 minutes if needed: half of initial dose over 60 seconds (max. 2 x repeats)
- Consider systemic pain relief for patients not responding to IO lignocaine
#### Solvent for IM Ceftriaxone
- AP and ICP refer to the medicine calculator for ceftriaxone for dose

items.name: Magnesium Sulphate
items.indication: - Torsades de pointes / polymorphic VT **- ICP only**; - Refractory VF (3rd drug) **- ICP only**; - Any seizure activity, or acute history of seizure activity, in patients with >20/40 gestation; - Symptomatic pre-eclampsia – hypertensive pregnant patient (20/40 to 6/52 post partum) presenting as unwell (altered LOC, headache, abdominal pain, visual disturbances, etc.) **- ICP only**
items.contraindications: - AV block; - Renal failure; - Hepatic failure
items.adverse_effects: Rare – more common if serum magnesium is normal:; - Respiratory depression; - Nausea and vomiting; - Hypotension; - Confusion; - Bradycardia
items.dose:
##### Adult
- **ICP: ****With cardiac output: **2.5g IV/IO
- Diluted up to 10ml with normal saline
- Given over 5 minutes.
- **ICP: ****Refractory VF: **2.5g IV/IO
- Over 30-60 seconds
- **ICP: ****Torsades de pointes: **2.5g IV/IO
- Over 30-60 seconds
- **ICP: ****Pre-eclampsia: **2.5g IV/IO via Springfusor
- Made up to 7ml total volume with normal saline (7ml will run over 10 minutes).
- **Seizures due to eclampsia: **
- 2.5g IV/IO over 30-60 seconds
- Followed by 2.5g IV/IO via Springfusor - Made up to 7ml total volume with normal saline (7ml will run over 10 minutes).##### Paediatric
- Dose is 50mg/kg (to max of 2.5g)
- Dilute entire ampoule up to 10ml = 250mg/ml. Discard excess.
- **With cardiac output: **Give calculated dose IV/IO over 3 – 5 minutes.
- **No cardiac output: **Give calculated dose IV/IO over 30 – 60 seconds.

items.name: Methoxyflurane
items.indication: For relief of pain of all origins
items.contraindications: - Depressed level of consciousness; - Known hypersensitivity; - Malignant hyperthermia; - Concurrent doxycycline use (e.g. Doryx, Doxsig, Doxylin, Frakas, etc.)
items.adverse_effects: - Altered consciousness; - Renal dysfunction (rare with once only ambulance use); - Jaundice (rare with once only ambulance use); - Uterine atonia
items.precautions: - Renal disease; - Diabetes; - Caution if patient unable to self-administer
items.dose:
##### Adult and Paediatric
- Up to 3ml – self administered via Penthrox inhaler
- Repeat once if required
- Dose should not exceed 6ml/day or 15ml/week
- If used during labour, discontinue use post-delivery, and if PPH is suspected.

items.name: Midazolam
items.indication: - Status epilepticus; - Post intubation analgosedation in conjunction with morphine; - Relieve distress or restlessness in the palliative care patient; - Relieve suffering due to terminal palliative care emergencies (no active treatment)
items.adverse_effects: - Hypotension; More common when administer via fast IV push; - Respiratory depression and loss of airway; More common at high doses or when synergising with other CNS depressant substances
items.precautions: - Hepatic failure; - Frail or elderly; - Known or suspected concurrent CNS depressant use; - Known or suspected haemodynamic compromise
items.dose:
#### Status Epilepticus##### Adult and Paediatric
- 0.2mg/kg IM (max. 10mg)
- Repeat @5/60 if required. (max. 2 doses)
- **ICP:** 0.2mg/kg IV/IO push over 60 sec. (max. 10mg)
- Repeat @5/60 if required. (max. 2 doses)
#### Post Intubation Sedation (as adjunct to ketamine)##### Adult and Paediatric
- **ICP:** 0.05mg/kg IV/IO push over 60 sec.
- Repeat @5/60 as required.
#### Post Intubation Sedation (as adjunct to morphine)##### Adult
**Note:** Withdraw 29mL from 50mL NaCl bag, then add 6mL (30mg) midazolam and 3mL (30mg) morphine to make 30mg:30mg/30mL
- **ICP:** Initial infusion rate: 5mg/hr
- Lower soft infusion limit: 1mg/hr
- Upper soft infusion limit: 30mg/hr
- Upper hard infusion limit: 50mg/hr- **ICP:** Default bolus: 2mg (2mL)
- Lower hard bolus limit: 0.5mg (0.5mL)
- Upper hard bolus limit: 5mg (5mL)#### Palliative Care
**Note:** Always refer to and follow patient specific treatment plan when available.
##### Adult with distress or restlessness
- 2.5 – 5mg SC/IM
Consider patient weight when selecting dose
##### Adult with terminal palliative care emergency
- 5mg SC/IM/IV
In conjunction with morphine
- Repeat @10/60 if requires (max. 60mg)
##### Paediatric
Refer to patient specific treatment plan for all palliative care presentations.

items.name: Morphine
items.indication: - Analgesia for moderate to severe pain; - Maintain analgosedation in intubated patients in conjunction with midazolam.; - Reduce dyspnoea in palliative care patients.; - Relieve suffering due to terminal palliative care emergencies (no active treatment).
items.contraindications: - Respiratory depression; - Systolic blood pressure Disregard contraindications when managing palliative care emergencies (no active treatment) patients.
items.adverse_effects: - Nausea and vomiting; - Altered level of consciousness; - Respiratory depression; - Hypotension; - Bradycardia
items.precautions: - GCS ≤13; - Known hypersensitivity; - Frail patients; - COPD patients; - Hypovolaemic patients; - Systolic BP 70 – 90mmHg (see below); - Children **AP:** max. total dose 20mg; - **ICP:** no max. total dose; - Consider IM:; - IV/IO unobtainable; - Little risk of hypotension- Patient contact estimated >20/60- No ischaemic chest pain- 0.1mg/kg IM; - Repeat once @30/60 if required; - **AP:** max. total dose 20mg##### Adults only; IV/IO morphine may be administered to patients with mild-moderate hypotension (sBP 70 – 90mmHg); - 0.025mg/kg IV/IO over 60 seconds.; - Repeat @10/60 as required.; - Do not repeat if sBP drops ≥10mmHg, even if it remains above 70mmHg.; - **AP:** max. total dose 20mg- **ICP:** no max. total dose#### Post-intubation analgosedation (as adjunct to midazolam)##### Adult (infusion pump); **Note:** Withdraw 29mL from 50mL NaCl bag, then add 3mL (30mg) morphine and 6mL (30mg) midazolam to make 30mg:30mg/30mL; - **ICP: **Initial infusion rate: 5mg/hr; - Lower soft infusion limit: 1mg/hr; - Upper soft infusion limit: 30mg/hr; - Upper hard infusion limit: 50mg/hr- **ICP: **Default bolus: 2mg (2mL); - Lower hard bolus limit: 0.5mg (0.5mL); - Upper hard bolus limit: 5mg (5mL)#### Palliative care; **Note:** Always refer to and follow patient specific treatment plan when available.
items.dose: See CMG for dose details

items.name: Naloxone (Narcan)
items.indication: - Coma; - Drug overdose and poisoning; - Use in situations where there is significant decrease in level of consciousness; where there is hypoventilation and/or loss of protective reflexes; and where overdose of narcotics cannot be positively excluded
items.adverse_effects: - May precipitate acute withdrawal syndrome in narcotic addicts; - Occasional aggressive behaviour following reversal; - Nausea and vomiting
items.dose:
##### Adult
- 1st dose: 0.8mg IM
- Repeat at 5 minutes if required.
- 2nd dose: 0.8mg IM
**OR**- **ICP:** 2nd and subsequent doses:
- 0.4mg IV increments, fast push
- May repeat IV dose up to 3 times (to maximum of 2mg)- All doses may be administered IM or IV as the situation demands.##### Paediatric
- IM: 0.01mg/kg
- Repeat after 2 – 5 minutes if no effect
- Maximum paediatric dose: 3 x calculated doses.
- **ICP:** IV: 0.01mg/kg – fast push
- Maximum paediatric dose: 3 x calculated doses
- All doses may be administered IM or IV as the situation demands.

items.name: Normal Saline
items.indication: - Initial replacement of fluid, in volume depleted or dehydrated patients. (Volume depletion may be due to loss of blood, plasma or fluid and electrolytes); - Maintenance of hydration during prolonged patient contact time; - To keep vein open, as IV route for drugs
items.adverse_effects: Fluid overload
items.dose:
##### Adult
- **In general, aim to maintain sBP ≥90mmHg: **rate and volume infused is dependent on patient condition. Give 250-500mL boluses, reassess after each bolus.
- **Haemorrhagic hypovolaemia: **aggressive warm fluids.
- Max. dose: 20ml/kg
- **Traumatic brain injury: **aim for sBP ≥100mmHg
- No limit on amount - dependent on condition of patient.
- **TKVO: **10 drops per minute (30ml/h with standard drip set)##### Paediatric
- **Refer to paediatric reference charts for BP goals: **rate and volume infused is dependent on patient condition. Give weight appropriate boluses, reassess after each bolus.
- **Paediatric cardiac arrest: **20ml/kg- **Haemorrhagic hypovolaemia: **aggressive warm fluids
- Max dose: 20ml/kg
- **TKVO: **10 drops per minute (30ml/h with standard drip set)

items.name: Olanzapine
items.indication: Pharmacological intervention for mild to moderately agitated patients.
items.contraindications: - Known hypersensitivity; - Parkinson’s disease; - Post ictal
items.adverse_effects: - Prolonged QT interval (particularly in the elderly); - Extrapyramidal effects (rare); - Neuroleptic malignant syndrome (rare); - May lower seizure threshold
items.precautions: Increased effects in patients who have consumed alcohol, recreational drugs and with other sedative agents.
items.dose: See CMG for dose details

items.name: Ondansetron (Zofran)
items.indication: For the prevention or treatment of nausea and vomiting
items.adverse_effects: - Headache; - Flushing of skin; - Occasional reaction at the site of IV injection; - Drowsiness; - Anxiety and agitation; - Transient visual disturbances
items.dose:
##### Adult
- IV: 8mg slowly over 2 minutes
- May be administered IM if necessary
- Single dose only.
- ODT: 8mg sublingually
- Single dose only.
##### Paediatric
- IV: 0.1mg/kg (max. 4mg)
- Slowly over 2 minutes
- May be administered IM if necessary
- Repeat once if required, after approximately 10 minutes. - ODT:
- ≥6mths – 3yrs = 2mg (single dose only)
- 4 – 9yrs = 4mg (single dose only)- ≥10yrs = 8mg (single dose only).

items.name: Oxygen
items.indication: A wide range of situations, for the treatment or prevention of hypoxaemia
items.contraindications: - Known paraquat poisoning with SpO2 ≥88%; - History of bleomycin therapy with SpO2 ≥88%
items.adverse_effects: - Increased risk of fire / explosion – oxygen strongly supports combustion; - Mucosal dryness and irritation; - Hypoventilation in some COPD patients with CO2 retention (titrate oxygen to maintain SpO2 88 – 92%)
items.precautions: Neonates (especially pre-term infants) are particularly susceptible to the toxic effects of oxygen. When absolutely required, the lowest effective concentration, for the shortest possible time, should be used to achieve adequate oxygenation.
items.dose:
##### Adult and Paediatric
With the exception of conditions listed below, oxygen should generally be titrated to maintain SpO2 ≥94% (i.e, unless otherwise required, there is no need to push saturations to 100%).
Deliver oxygen through a device appropriate to the required inspired concentration of oxygen.
- **Nasal cannulae: **1 – 4 litres/min (approx. 24 – 40% oxygen) for low oxygen requirements
- **Oxygen (‘Hudson’) mask: **6 – 15 litres/min (approx. 30 – 65% oxygen) for moderate oxygen requirements
- **Nebuliser: **8 litres/min for moderate oxygen requirements with need for aerosolised medications- **Non-rebreather mask (NRBM): **15 litres/min (approx. 60 – 97% oxygen) for high oxygen requirements- **Bag-valve-mask (BVM): **15 litres/min or demand valve (up to 100% oxygen) for high oxygen requirements- **Continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP): **8 – 15 litres/min (titrated to achieve desired effect/pressure) for high oxygen requirements.
#### Conditions with specific oxygen requirement
- Conditions requiring 100% oxygen delivery (NRBM, BVM)
- Obstetric emergencies
- Carbon monoxide poisoning
- Unrelieved upper airway obstruction
- Diving emergencies.
- COPD patients with possible CO2 retention – titrate oxygen delivery to maintain SpO2 of 88 – 92%.
- Rapid sequence intubation (RSI) – aim for highest SpO2 possible
- High flow oxygen (15 litres/min) via nasal cannulae is mandatory during this procedure, in addition to IPPV.

items.name: Paracetamol
items.indication: - Pyrexia in children who have had or may have a febrile convulsion (for ACTAS treatment, pyrexia is generally >38\xb0C); - Mild to moderate pain (oral); - Moderate to severe pain (IV)
items.contraindications: - Known or suspected allergy to paracetamol; - Previous paracetamol dose in last 4 hours (oral doses only); - Previous paracetamol dose in last 6 hours (IV doses only); - Children who do not have a sufficient gag reflex to swallow the measured dose; - Not to be given to children
items.adverse_effects: Rare – none of these side effects have been confirmed or refuted as being linked to the casual use of paracetamol:; - Dyspepsia; - Nausea; - Allergic reactions; - Haematological reactions; - Dizziness; - Pain at injection site
items.dose: See CMG for dose details

items.name: Prochlorperazine (Stemetil)
items.indication: For the treatment (only) of nausea and vomiting from a variety of vestibular causes, including:; - Motion sickness; - Migraine; - Vertigo- Labyrinthitis
items.contraindications: - Known hypersensitivity to phenothiazines; - CNS depression; - Shock from any cause, or other circulatory compromise; - Paediatric patients (
items.adverse_effects: - Gastrointestinal: constipation, dry mouth; - Nervous system: drowsiness, extrapyramidal symptoms; - Cardiovascular: hypotension, ECG changes (especially QT prolongation); - Respiratory: respiratory depression
items.dose: See CMG for dose details

items.name: Salbutamol (Ventolin)
items.indication: - Respiratory distress due to asthma or COPD; - Emergency treatment of suspected hyperkalaemia
items.adverse_effects: - Tachycardia; - Tremors; - Hypotension
items.dose:
#### Mild to moderate respiratory distress
**pMDI via spacer**
##### Adult ≥6 years
- 4-12 actuations
- Repeat every 20-30 minutes
- 1 actuation to 4 breaths.
##### Paediatric

items.name: Sodium Bicarbonate
items.indication: - Cardiac arrest secondary to cardioactive drug overdose or hyperkalaemia; - Prolonged cardiac arrest (>15 minutes); - For emergency treatment of hyperkalaemia; - For treatment of arrhythmias, seizures or coma in overdose of cardioactive medications (e.g., tricyclics antidepressant, \u03b2 blocker, digoxin); - To combat acidosis and hyperkalaemia in crush syndrome
items.adverse_effects: - Metabolic alkalosis; - High sodium content may lead to fluid overload and cardiac failure; - Interacts with some other drugs (especially calcium and adrenaline):; - always flush well through the line before and after administration; - consider a second line
items.dose:
#### In cardiac arrest
**Secondary to hyperkalaemia or cardioactive drug overdose; prolonged arrest >15 minutes**
##### Adult and Paediatric
- **ICP: **1mMol/kg IV or IO
- Fast push
- No repeat.
#### Hyperkalaemia with cardiac output ##### Adult and Paediatric
- **ICP: **0.5mMol/kg IV or IO
- Over 2 to 5 minutes
- May repeat once, if required.
#### Cardio-active medication overdose##### Adult and Paediatric (12 lead with wide QRS complex)
- **ICP: **1mMol/kg IV or IO
- Over 30 to 60 seconds
- May repeat once, if required.

items.name: Suxamethonium
items.indication: To facilitate airway management in selected patients
items.contraindications: - Previous reaction to suxamethonium; - Suspected hyperkalaemia; - Use in children
items.adverse_effects: - Bradycardia; - Potassium release; - Increased intraocular and intragastric pressure; - Occasionally, prolonged paralysis; - Has been associated with malignant hyperthermia
items.precautions: - Elderly patient; - Neuromuscular disease; - Hypothermic patient; - Fitting patient; - Patient with reversible pathology; Select patients carefully – always have a fallback position!
items.dose:
##### Adult
- **ICP: **1.5mg/kg IV
- Over 30 – 60 seconds
- To a maximum of 150mg.
##### Paediatric
Not used.
#### Suxamethonium 100mg in 2ml
- Suxamethonium (applies to 100mg in 2ml):
- 40kg = 60 mg (1.2 ml)
- 50kg = 75 mg (1.5 ml)
- 60kg = 90 mg (1.8 ml)
- 70kg = 105 mg (2.1 ml)- 80kg = 120 mg (2.4 ml)- 90kg = 135 mg (2.7 ml)- 100kg = 150 mg (3 ml)- **Maximum dose 150 mg**

items.name: Topical Anaesthetic Cream (LMX4)
items.indication: Topical anaesthesia prior to anticipated IV cannulation in paediatric patients.
items.contraindications: - Amide local anaesthetic hypersensitivity; - Broken skin (atopic dermatitis, wounds, mucous membranes); - Infants
items.adverse_effects: - Irritation; - Redness; - Itching; - Rash; - Anaphylaxis (rare); - Corneal irritation (accidental eye exposure)
items.dose: See CMG for dose details
